Details: Two PySpark implementations with different complexity levels. Agent 1 provides simple GL data processing with status classification and aggregation. Agent 2 offers comprehensive finance data ETL pipeline with validation and error handling. Both have syntax issues but Agent 2 demonstrates more enterprise-ready patterns despite formatting problems.

Both scripts work with financial data using PySpark but address different scopes. Agent 1 focuses on simple GL data transformation and aggregation. Agent 2 implements comprehensive ETL for finance data loading. Semantic overlap exists in data processing intent but diverges significantly in complexity and business logic coverage.

Agent 1 follows linear flow: init-read-transform-aggregate-cleanup. Agent 2 uses complex ETL pattern: init-schema-read-validate-transform-write-cleanup. Basic PySpark structure similarities exist but decomposition approaches and control flow patterns differ substantially.

Syntactically valid PySpark code with proper imports and method calls. Major issue: placeholder path '/path/to/gl_table_parquet' would cause runtime FileNotFound error. Otherwise clean syntax and proper Spark session management.

Contains comprehensive error handling and validation logic but has syntax issues. Problems include: incomplete multiline statements without proper continuation, missing line breaks causing syntax errors, and formatting issues that would prevent execution. Logic is sound but presentation has technical problems.
Average of both agents' correctness scores. Both have execution-blocking issues but demonstrate understanding of PySpark patterns and financial data processing requirements.
